speaker: Draymond Green
prediction_text: The New York Knicks will beat the Philadelphia 76ers in six games in the conference semifinals.
argument: Rest edge: New York had enough rest to be refreshed without getting rusty; Embiid matchup strain: Karl-Anthony Towns pulling Joel Embiid away from the rim would make the matchup harder on Embiid's body; Defensive depth: The Knicks had more defenders to throw at Tyrese Maxey than Philadelphia had to throw at Jalen Brunson; Foul pressure: Philadelphia's best chance was to force Towns into foul trouble by making him guard Embiid.

speaker: Draymond Green
prediction_text: The San Antonio Spurs will beat the Minnesota Timberwolves in six games in the conference semifinals.
argument: Health concerns: Anthony Edwards' health and Donte DiVincenzo being out made him unwilling to pick Minnesota; Defensive plan: Minnesota would need unusual defensive coverages on Victor Wembanyama and Stephon Castle, showing the matchup difficulty; Castle importance: He said Stephon Castle was the key to San Antonio.

speaker: Draymond Green
prediction_text: The Detroit Pistons will beat the Cleveland Cavaliers in six games in the conference semifinals.
argument: Momentum: Detroit's first-round comeback would make the team band together; Schroder matchup: Dennis Schroder could swing the series for Cleveland if Detroit did not match his physicality and mind games; Expectation: He explicitly said the Pistons were supposed to win the series.

speaker: Draymond Green
prediction_text: The Oklahoma City Thunder will beat the Los Angeles Lakers in no more than five games and return to the conference finals.
argument: Season dominance: Oklahoma City went 4-0 against the Lakers and the games were not close; Roster edge: He did not see the Lakers beating the best team in the NBA without Luka Doncic, even with Jalen Williams out for Oklahoma City; Matchup problem: He thought LeBron James guarding Chet Holmgren might only give the Lakers a chance to steal one game.
